top_categories.workout_context: 54ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At mile 60 of 102, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
At Gran Fondo Jasper, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Gran Fondo Jasper. Train it until it feels boring.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 102 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
5,548ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Gran Fondo Jasper's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before June.
5,548ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
Gran Fondo Jasper's paved mountain highway through Jasper National forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 102 miles.
102 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 102 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 5,548ft of climbing on paved mountain highway through Jasper National does to your power file on race day.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Gran Fondo Jasper’s 102 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Gran Fondo Jasper. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on TT Threshold, Durability, and Over Under to prepare you for 102 miles of paved mountain highway through jasper national park in Jasper, Alberta, Canada.
